company_type: Regulated Electric and Natural Gas Utility

dependencies: Significant capital investment requirements for utility operations
Dependence on suppliers to deliver components in accordance with short and long-term project schedules
Large global demand for energy-related infrastructure has stretched equipment supply chains
Extended delivery dates and increased prices for items like combustion turbines, transformers and other large electrical equipment
Labor market for skilled engineering and construction resources strained
Scarcity of certain raw materials and interruptions in production and shipping
Inflationary pressures, geopolitical events and federal policies exacerbating supply chain situation
risks: Shortage of key components where alternative supplier is not identified could significantly impact operations and project plans
Supply chain constraints may impact capital expenditures and ability to obtain financing on favorable terms
Disruptions in imports from key suppliers could impact project timelines and costs
Tariffs and trade complaints may impact procurement activities and project costs
Inability to secure sufficient insurance coverage or increased costs of insurance

regulatory_risks: Changes in regulation may impair ability to recover costs from customers
Adverse regulatory rulings or imposition of additional regulations could negatively impact results
FERC can impose penalties of up to $1.5 million per violation per day for energy trading activities
NERC electric reliability standards and critical infrastructure protection requirements subject to potential financial penalties
PHMSA authority to assess penalties for pipeline safety violations
State agencies have authority to impose substantial penalties
Legislative and regulatory responses to climate change may create financial risk
EPA regulations addressing control of CO2 emissions from power sector
EPA proposed air regulations for municipal waste combustors
EPA rules designating certain PFAS as hazardous substances under CERCLA
EPA rules setting enforceable drinking water standards for certain PFAS
EPA rules under Clean Water Act setting Effluent Limitations Guidelines for steam generating coal plants
Colorado Senate Bill 23-291 establishing fuel cost mechanisms and natural gas planning rules
Colorado Senate Bill 24-218 accelerating investment in electric distribution
Changes in environmental laws and regulations may result in substantial costs
Federal Clean Water Act Section 316(b) requirements for cooling water intake structures
EPA Good Neighbor provisions establishing NOx allowance trading program
CCR Rule imposing requirements for handling, storage, treatment and disposal of coal ash
Amendments to CCR Rule widening scope to include legacy surface impoundments and areas where CCR was placed on land
State regulatory commissions impose dividend limitations and capital structure requirements
Issuance of securities by utility subsidiaries subject to state regulatory commission and/or FERC jurisdiction
Rate regulation premised on providing opportunity to earn reasonable rate of return on invested capital
Regulatory commissions authorize ROE, capital structure and depreciation rates in rate proceedings
Decisions by regulators can significantly impact results of operations and credit quality
Public perception of high commodity prices passed through to customer bills could impact ability to recover costs
Regulatory changes or limitations related to use of natural gas as energy source

sanction_risks: Prohibiting Russian Uranium Imports Act signed into law in May 2024 - NSP-Minnesota no longer permitted to accept deliveries of enriched nuclear material from Russia beginning August 2024 unless specific waivers requested and received
Tariffs and trade complaints may impact procurement activities including company owned projects and PPAs
Executive orders issued in early 2025 imposing new tariffs on certain imports which may impact procurement activities
Policy actions or restrictions on solar and storage imports could impact project timelines and costs
